title: SEC2: double JSON parse of WS notifications
description:
File: wasm/src/transport.rs line 72, wasm/src/client.rs line 127.

Every server-push notification is parsed twice: once in transport.rs onmessage (serde_json::from_str at line 72) to dispatch response vs. notification, and again in the notify_closure in client.rs (serde_json::from_str at line 127) to extract method and params.

Cost: two heap-allocating JSON parses + two serde_json::Value trees per notification message. For busy rooms (many user_joined/deliver events) this is measurable latency per message.

Fix: transport.rs should pass the already-parsed Value to the notify callback instead of passing the raw string. Change the notify_cb type from js_sys::Function (called with raw string) to a Rust closure that accepts serde_json::Value, so the client closure receives the pre-parsed value directly. This eliminates the second parse entirely.

Classify: P2 (measurable per-message latency in hot path)
